content.claim: A 2003 amendment extending SEN categories to include autism and learning difficulties from psychiatric developmental disorders was followed by a fivefold increase in learners classified under this new category within five years, with significant inter-county variation, prompting a full population review and rule amendment in 2007.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Demonstrates that definitional change alone can drive apparent surges independent of true prevalence
Does not isolate the relative contribution of definitional change to current (post-2015) identification trends
Historical precedent does not prove current rise is solely definitional
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
